text: The surgeon inserts a visualized trocar port to safely access the abdomen, revealing the peritoneum. During diagnostic laparoscopy, a single adhesion band is identified, extending from the transverse colon to the mesentery, causing obstruction at the proximal ileum.

The surgeon performs sharp dissection using scissors to safely lyse the adhesion band. The entire intestine is inspected from the duodenal junction to the ileocecal valve, ensuring no heat damage to the bowel.

The surgeon encounters a complex adhesion between the omentum and the abdominal wall. Trocar ports are strategically placed away from previous surgical scars to minimize the risk of injury to intra-abdominal organs.

During the diagnostic laparoscopy, multiple adhesion bands are identified as the cause of obstruction. The surgeon excises these bands using cold scissors, while optimal retraction of the bowel is maintained with an atraumatic grasper. Energy devices are avoided to prevent thermal injury.
